groups.title: OLD TIMEY SLANG FOR LAW ENFORCEMENT
groups.explanation: OLD TIMEY SLANG FOR LAW ENFORCEMENT

hints: Outer layers and coverings tied to the body, from hard protective surfaces to soft external growth.
Each appears in classic idioms that signal huge quantity, overwhelming scale, or impossible-to-count amounts.
Vintage street-slang labels for police or detectives; think noir dialogue and old crime novels.
Wordplay at the front: each longer entry begins with a different synonym of "throw" before veering elsewhere.
